description: Add E2E tests verifying the side panel no-flicker fix (pluginsLoaded gate, await-before-loading-false, stale-while-revalidating on reconnect) and fix the 2 pre-existing broken E2E tests in onboarding.e2e.ts and side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts.

userStories.title: Fix pre-existing E2E failures in onboarding and side-panel-live-update tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the 2 pre-existing E2E test failures to be fixed so that the full E2E suite passes cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts 'adding a plugin transitions the side panel from no-plugins to plugin list' passes
e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts 'side panel updates plugin list when config.json adds a plugin' passes
All other existing E2E tests continue to pass (no regressions)
userStories.notes:
Two E2E tests fail on the current main branch. Both fail at the same step: after writing a new plugin to config.json, calling POST /reload, and waiting, the side panel does not transition from the no-plugins state to the plugin list within the timeout.

Failing tests:
1. e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts:82 — 'adding a plugin transitions the side panel from no-plugins to plugin list'
   - Line 119: `await expect(sidePanelPage.locator('text=No Plugins Installed')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 30_000 });` — times out
   - Line 120: `await expect(sidePanelPage.locator('button[aria-expanded]')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });` — also fails
2. e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts:53 — 'side panel updates plugin list when config.json adds a plugin'
   - Line 92-93: Same pattern — No Plugins text stays visible, accordion button never appears

Both tests use the same pattern: start server with 0 plugins, open side panel, verify no-plugins state, write config.json with a plugin, POST /reload, then check that the plugin list appears.

The issue is that the side panel page opened via `chrome-extension://<id>/side-panel/side-panel.html` URL navigation (not the real side panel API) may not reliably receive chrome.runtime messages (sp:serverMessage, ws:message). The ws:message handler in App.tsx at line 243-249 catches sync.full and calls loadPlugins(), but message delivery to extension pages opened via URL is inconsistent in headless Chromium.

Diagnosis approach:
- Check if the side panel page's chrome.runtime.onMessage listener is receiving messages at all
- The postReload() triggers a sync.full which should reach the side panel via both sp:serverMessage (plugins.changed) AND ws:message (sync.full) paths
- Consider adding a polling mechanism: instead of relying solely on push notifications, have the test explicitly trigger a loadPlugins via page.evaluate or add a manual refresh mechanism
- Another approach: use Playwright's page.waitForFunction to poll the DOM for the accordion button, combined with triggering multiple reloads
- Check if the config watcher's performConfigReload and the manual POST /reload are both completing and sending sync.full

Key files:
- e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts:82-130 — the failing test
- e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts:53-103 — the other failing test
- e2e/helpers.ts:47-55 — openSidePanel helper (opens via URL navigation)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:224-251 — message listeners for sp:serverMessage and ws:message
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts — how messages are relayed to side panel

userStories.title: Add E2E test: side panel opens directly to plugin list without flash
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to open directly showing my installed plugins without a brief flash of 'No Plugins Installed' so that the UX feels smooth and confident.
acceptanceCriteria: New E2E test in e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts or a new e2e/side-panel-no-flicker.e2e.ts verifies the no-flash behavior
Test starts a server WITH plugins pre-configured, opens the side panel, and asserts that 'No Plugins Installed' is NEVER visible
Test verifies that plugin list (accordion button with plugin name) becomes visible within a reasonable timeout
The test uses Playwright's expect(...).not.toBeVisible() or polling to confirm the no-plugins text never appears during the loading-to-plugins transition
userStories.notes:
The core fix in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx introduces two mechanisms that prevent the flash:
1. `await loadPlugins()` in the init effect — `loading` stays true until plugin data arrives, so the LoadingState spinner shows until plugins are ready, then transitions directly to the plugin list
2. `pluginsLoaded` state gate — NoPluginsState only renders when the server has explicitly responded with 0 plugins, not when plugin data simply hasn't arrived yet

The test should verify scenario: server has plugins, open side panel, plugins appear without any intermediate 'No Plugins Installed' flash.

Implementation approach:
- Start MCP server with the e2e-test plugin registered (same as other tests)
- Launch extension context, wait for connection
- Open side panel via openSidePanel(context)
- Use a tight polling loop or MutationObserver via page.evaluate to watch for ANY appearance of 'No Plugins Installed' text in the DOM
- Simultaneously wait for the plugin accordion to become visible
- Assert that the 'No Plugins Installed' text was NEVER rendered at any point
- One approach: use page.evaluate to set up a MutationObserver before the page finishes loading that records whether the text ever appeared, then check the flag after the plugin list is visible

Key files:
- e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts — existing empty state tests, good place to add this or create new file
- e2e/fixtures.ts — test fixtures
- e2e/helpers.ts — openSidePanel, waitForExtensionConnected, etc.
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:316-354 — the render conditional that was changed

userStories.title: Add E2E test: reconnection preserves stale plugin list without flash
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to smoothly transition when reconnecting to the server (e.g., after a port change) without flashing 'No Plugins Installed' between the disconnect and reconnect states.
acceptanceCriteria: New E2E test verifies that during server restart (simulating reconnection), the side panel does NOT flash 'No Plugins Installed' between the disconnect and reconnect+plugins-loaded states

Test starts with server and plugins, verifies plugins visible, kills server, verifies disconnect state appears, restarts server, verifies plugins reappear WITHOUT any intermediate 'No Plugins Installed' flash
The disconnect state ('Cannot Reach MCP Server') is still correctly shown when the server is down
userStories.notes:
This tests the stale-while-revalidating aspect of the fix. The key behaviors:
1. When the server disconnects, the side panel shows DisconnectedState ('Cannot Reach MCP Server') — this is correct
2. When the server reconnects, the side panel should transition from DisconnectedState directly to PluginList — NOT through NoPluginsState
3. The `pluginsLoaded` state is reset to false on disconnect (App.tsx line 216), and plugins are cleared (line 217). On reconnect, loadPlugins() is called (line 214) and pluginsLoaded is set back to true only when the server responds.
4. During the gap between reconnect (connected=true) and loadPlugins resolving, the render hits the `null` branch (App.tsx line 354) because pluginsLoaded=false and hasContent=false — showing an empty main area instead of the NoPluginsState card.

Implementation approach:
- Start MCP server with the e2e-test plugin
- Open side panel, verify plugin is visible
- Set up a DOM observer (via page.evaluate with MutationObserver) to watch for any appearance of 'No Plugins Installed'
- Kill the server
- Verify 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' appears
- Start a new server on the SAME port with the same secret and plugin config
- Wait for plugins to reappear
- Check the observer flag — 'No Plugins Installed' should NEVER have appeared

Note: Restarting a server on the same port requires using startMcpServer with the same configDir. The extension's offscreen WebSocket has exponential backoff reconnection — it will automatically reconnect when the new server starts.

Key files:
- e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts:132-167 — existing 'disconnected state shows when server stops' test, good pattern to follow
- e2e/fixtures.ts — startMcpServer
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:208-223 — the sp:connectionState handler
- platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts — WebSocket reconnection logic
